problemStatement: Pantagruel's AST uses string-named binders in EForall/EExists/EEach (and, indirectly, in rule declarations and comprehension guards). Capture-avoiding substitution, priming, free-variable collection, and (post-PR-#110) alpha-renaming are implemented by a family of hand-rolled AST walkers in lib/smt_expr.ml, lib/smt_doc.ml, and lib/smt.ml. These walkers have a documented latent capture bug in substitute_vars: they filter the substitution's domain by bound names but do not alpha-rename the binder when the substitution's range contains a name that becomes captured. PR #110 added rename_var_refs, rename_guards, and alpha_rename_binders to patch one symptom; the underlying design problem — substitution correctness distributed across bespoke walkers — remains. Issue #111 proposes migrating binder handling to a proper library so correctness comes from a well-tested primitive and the whole helper family collapses.
solutionSummary: Adopt the Bindlib library as the AST's binder abstraction and rewrite the walker family as thin wrappers over its primitives. Phase 1 adds the opam dependency and a thin adapter module (Pantagruel.Binder) that re-exports Bindlib's Var/Binder/Mbinder types under project-local names — this seam also contains the Bindlib-vs-locally-nameless choice, so Patch 3's AST reshape is insulated from that decision. Phase 2 reshapes Ast.expr so that EForall/EExists/EEach carry a Bindlib mbinder instead of a (param list * expr) pair, hand-writes show/equal instances for the three affected variants (Bindlib's mbinder is opaque to ppx_deriving, and we want alpha-aware equality anyway), and updates every consumer (parser, pretty-printer, check, collect, JSON/markdown output, normalize, SMT pipeline) to construct binders via bind_mvar and destructure via unmbind. The substitute_vars / prime_expr / free_vars rewrites collapse to one-line calls into Bindlib; collect_body_guards keeps its logic but delegates scope tracking to unmbind. Patch 5 retires PR #110's alpha-rename helpers (rename_var_refs, rename_guards, alpha_rename_binders) — the library subsumes them. Behavior on existing fixtures is preserved; the latent capture bug is closed automatically by the library.
currentStateAnalysis: On master (post PR #110 merge, 2026-04-22): lib/smt_expr.ml (~476 LOC) defines substitute_vars, substitute_guards, prime_expr, prime_guards, unprime_expr, collect_body_guards, and — newly from #110 — rename_var_refs and rename_guards as mutually-recursive walkers over Ast.expr. lib/smt.ml (~2164 LOC) defines alpha_rename_binders at line 17 and invokes it at line 743 to patch SMT binder capture when a quantifier binder shadows a declared rule. lib/smt_doc.ml defines free_vars and bind_head_params, both of which hand-roll scope tracking using StringSet. lib/smt.ml `include`s Smt_expr so the walkers' public API leaks into the Smt module's surface. The AST (lib/ast.ml) stores binder params as `param list` and guards as `guard list`; binder names are plain strings carried in Lower/Upper identifier wrappers; every type has `[@@deriving show, eq]`. No Bindlib dependency is declared in pantagruel.opam. The opam repository has bindlib 6.0.0 available (OCaml >= 4.07; dune >= 2.7; compatible with the project's OCaml 5.4.1 switch).

mergabilityStrategy.featureFlagStrategy: No feature flag. This is an internal refactor of the AST's binder representation. Observable behavior of `pant`, `pant --json`, `pant --check`, and `pant --normalize` on every existing fixture must be preserved byte-for-byte (modulo the latent capture bug, which no fixture exercises today). Snapshot tests in test/snapshots/ gate against unintended output drift. The migration is staged over five patches so that each patch is independently mergeable and revertable — the AST reshape (Patch 3) is the atomic cut-over point.
mergabilityStrategy.patchOrderingStrategy: Early (Patch 1): add library dependency and adapter module — no semantic impact. Early (Patch 2): add pending test stubs for capture-avoidance properties — compiles but pending. Middle (Patch 3): atomic AST reshape + walker rewrites + consumer updates — one reviewable but large diff; behavior preserved. Middle (Patch 4): un-skip and implement the tests from Patch 2, confirming capture-avoidance. Late (Patch 5): retire redundant helpers (rename_var_refs / alpha_rename_binders from PR #110 if merged; simplify bind_head_params to use Bindlib's free-vars). Every patch is INFRA because the user-visible behavior of existing fixtures is preserved.

requiredChanges.description: Add `bindlib` to the depends list. Generated from dune-project, so the real edit is in dune-project.
Add `bindlib` to the (depends ...) stanza for the pantagruel package.
New adapter module wrapping Bindlib's Var/Binder/Mbinder primitives under project-local names.
requiredChanges.signature:
module Var : sig type 'a t val make : string -> 'a t val name : 'a t -> string end
module Mbinder : sig type ('a, 'b) t val bind : 'a Var.t array -> 'b -> ('a, 'b) t val unbind : ('a, 'b) t -> 'a Var.t array * 'b val subst : ('a, 'b) t -> 'a array -> 'b val free_vars : ('a -> 'a Var.t list) -> ('a, 'b) t -> 'a Var.t list end

requiredChanges.description: Interface file for lib/binder.ml exposing only the adapter API (not raw Bindlib types).

Reshape EForall / EExists / EEach so the binder carries an mbinder. Keep `param` for parser-side surface (name + type_expr) but introduce a `param_meta` type that travels alongside the mbinder so the pretty-printer can recover the user-written name and type annotation. Drop `[@@deriving show, eq]` at the `expr` level (Bindlib's mbinder is opaque to ppx_deriving) and hand-write `equal_expr` / `pp_expr` that unbind for structural + alpha-aware comparison.
requiredChanges.signature:
type expr = ... | EForall of (expr, guarded_body) Binder.Mbinder.t * param_meta list | EExists of ... | EEach of (expr, guarded_body) Binder.Mbinder.t * param_meta list * combiner option
and guarded_body = guard list * expr
val equal_expr : expr -> expr -> bool
val pp_expr : Format.formatter -> expr -> unit

requiredChanges.description: Update quantifier / comprehension productions to build mbinders via Binder.Mbinder.bind after collecting params.
Update quantifier printing to call Binder.Mbinder.unbind, recover param metadata, and print the user-facing name + type annotation.

Rewrite substitute_vars, substitute_guards, prime_expr, prime_guards as thin wrappers over Binder.Mbinder.subst / map_binder. collect_body_guards continues to walk the expression but uses unbind when it descends into quantifiers.
requiredChanges.signature:
val substitute_vars : (string * expr) list -> expr -> expr
val prime_expr : ?bound:string list -> expr -> expr
val collect_body_guards : ?bound:string list -> Env.t -> expr -> expr list

requiredChanges.description: Rewrite free_vars as a wrapper over Binder.Mbinder.free_vars. Simplify bind_head_params to use the rewritten free_vars (semantics unchanged).
requiredChanges.signature:
val free_vars : expr -> StringSet.t
val bind_head_params : ?exclude:StringSet.t -> param list -> expr located -> expr located

requiredChanges.description: No logic changes expected — the `include Smt_expr` line preserves the public API. Call sites that pattern-match on EForall/EExists/EEach must be updated to destructure the mbinder.
Update every `match expr with | EForall ...` to call Binder.Mbinder.unbind and thread the bound names into the local env.
Same as check.ml — update match arms on quantifier constructors.
Update JSON emission for quantifiers to use unbind; preserve the user-facing param names in the emitted JSON so `pant --json` output is byte-for-byte identical on existing fixtures.
Same as json_output.ml — emit user-facing names via unbind.
Update normal-form rewrites on quantifiers to use unbind/bind.

New test file. QCheck2 properties: substitution identity when var is not free; capture-avoidance in the substitute [x -> y] (all y:T | x) case; alpha-equivalence preservation under substitution; free_vars matches legacy implementation on the full regression fixture corpus.
Add test_smt_substitution to the test executables.

acceptanceCriteria: bindlib is declared in dune-project and pulled in by opam install . --deps-only.
lib/binder.ml adapter module exists and exposes Var.t / Mbinder.t / bind_mvar / unbind / subst / free_vars under project-local names.
Ast.expr's EForall / EExists / EEach carry a Bindlib mbinder (no `param list` for the binder payload).
substitute_vars, prime_expr, free_vars are thin wrappers over Binder primitives — each under 20 LOC.
dune build produces no warnings and no errors across the default profile.
dune test passes unchanged on all existing regression fixtures (test/regression/*.pant) and all snapshot tests (test/snapshots/).
New test test_smt_substitution exercises the capture-avoidance property that was latent on master and passes.
rename_var_refs, rename_guards (lib/smt_expr.ml), and alpha_rename_binders (lib/smt.ml) are removed; no caller references them.
bind_head_params uses the rewritten free_vars (no hand-rolled scope tracking).
pant --json, pant --check, and pant --normalize produce byte-identical output on every fixture in test/ (no snapshot drift).

explicitOpinions.opinion: Pick Bindlib over locally-nameless, with locally-nameless as a documented fallback.
explicitOpinions.rationale: Bindlib is stable, maintained, and has non-trivial downstream users (Dedukti). Its abstraction surface is minimal — bind_var, unbind, subst, free_vars — and the correctness properties we want come from the library. Locally-nameless requires us to hand-roll the atom / index machinery and reprove its invariants.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Route all Bindlib usage through a project-local adapter module (lib/binder.ml) rather than sprinkling Bindlib calls across the codebase.
explicitOpinions.rationale: The adapter is a stable seam: if Bindlib's ppx ergonomics turn out poor and we need to fall back to locally-nameless, only lib/binder.ml changes. It also keeps the type signatures in ast.ml readable (Binder.Mbinder.t rather than the raw Bindlib.mbinder constructor).

explicitOpinions.opinion: Atomic AST reshape in a single patch (Patch 3) rather than a parallel dual-representation approach.
explicitOpinions.rationale: A parallel AST would require maintaining two representations and conversion functions, doubling the review surface and the testing burden for a refactor that has no user-visible intermediate states. The AST reshape is large but mechanical — every consumer descends through a small number of match arms. Reviewing one coherent patch is easier than reviewing the migration + the scaffolding + the removal across three commits.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Keep ts2pant's UniqueSupply / NameRegistry after the migration, even though Bindlib makes them redundant for correctness.
explicitOpinions.rationale: Their value shifts from correctness (was: belt) to readability (is: suspenders). Fresh hygienic names like `$1` still produce better pretty-printed output than Bindlib's default alpha-renaming choices, which prioritize freshness over readability.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Classify every patch as INFRA, not BEHAVIOR.
explicitOpinions.rationale: The issue explicitly states the migration is semantics-preserving on existing fixtures — the latent capture bug is not triggered by any current input. Fixing a bug that never manifests does not count as behavior change under the gameplan classification.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Hand-write equal_expr and pp_expr; drop `[@@deriving show, eq]` at the expr level.
explicitOpinions.rationale: Bindlib's ('a, 'b) mbinder is an opaque closure with no structural equality and no default printer. Autogenerated show/eq cannot descend into a binder. A ~30-line hand-rolled deriver that unbinds each quantifier variant gives us the right thing anyway — alpha-aware equality — which callers like test_parser.ml's round-trip tests already implicitly want.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Keep a locally-nameless fallback plan inside the adapter module's design.
explicitOpinions.rationale: If Bindlib's typing proves incompatible with some downstream consumer (most likely the pretty-printer or the JSON encoder), the fallback is a hand-rolled Chargueraud-style locally-nameless representation. Because every consumer calls through lib/binder.ml rather than Bindlib directly, swapping the implementation is a single-file change. Patch 1's adapter API is designed with both backends in mind — only primitives that both representations support are exposed.

patches.title: Add bindlib dependency and Binder adapter module

files.description: Add bindlib to the pantagruel package's depends list.
Regenerate via dune to include bindlib.
Add bindlib to (libraries ...).
Adapter module: Var (make/name), Mbinder (bind/unbind/subst/free_vars). Re-exports selected Bindlib primitives under stable local names.
Interface file constraining the adapter surface. Does not re-export raw Bindlib types.

changes: Add `bindlib` to the depends stanza in dune-project and regenerate pantagruel.opam (via `dune build` or manual sync).

Create lib/binder.ml with `module Var = Bindlib.Var`, `module Mbinder` wrapping Bindlib.mbinder / bind_mvar / unmbind / msubst / mbinder_arity / mbinder_names, plus a `free_vars : ('a -> 'a Var.t list) -> ('a, 'b) t -> 'a Var.t list` helper.

Restrict the adapter API to primitives that both Bindlib and a hand-rolled locally-nameless backend can support (Var.make/Var.name, Mbinder.bind/unbind/subst/free_vars, alpha-aware equality). This keeps the locally-nameless fallback a single-file swap if Bindlib proves incompatible downstream.
Create lib/binder.mli with the public surface only; do not re-export raw Bindlib types.
Add `bindlib` to lib/dune (libraries ...).
Run `dune build` to confirm the new module compiles.
patches.spec:
module BINDLIB_MIGRATION_PATCH_1.

> Patch 1 introduces the Binder adapter module wrapping Bindlib's
> Var and Mbinder primitives. No AST changes yet; no invariants
> asserted. The contribution is the adapter surface itself,
> available to subsequent patches.

Binder.
Var.
---
true.

patches.title: Add pending test stubs for capture-avoiding substitution

files.description: Alcotest + QCheck2 file declaring pending tests for substitute identity, capture-avoidance (substitute [x -> y] (all y:T | x)), alpha-equivalence preservation, and free_vars parity with the legacy implementation.
Register test_smt_substitution as a test executable.

changes: Create test/test_smt_substitution.ml with four tests tagged `PENDING: Patch 4` — each raises Failure "pending" so the test executable compiles and runs, but the tests themselves are skipped via Alcotest's `Skip` return.
Test 1: substitute_vars is the identity when the domain name is not free in e.

Test 2: substitute_vars [x -> EVar y] (EForall [y:T] (EVar x)) produces a quantifier alpha-equivalent to EForall [z:T] (EVar y) — the current implementation captures; the post-migration implementation must not.
Test 3: alpha-equivalent inputs produce alpha-equivalent outputs under substitute_vars.
Test 4: free_vars over the regression fixture corpus agrees with the legacy StringSet-based implementation (captured as a frozen baseline at stub time).
Register the new executable in test/dune.
testStubsIntroduced: Smt_substitution > substitute_vars is identity on non-free names
Smt_substitution > substitute_vars avoids capture in (all y:T | x) case
patches.spec:
module BINDLIB_MIGRATION_PATCH_2.

> Patch 2 introduces pending test stubs for the capture-avoidance
> properties that Patch 4 will activate. No invariants asserted
> yet — the stubs compile but are skipped.

Binder.
Var.
Expr.
---
true.

patches.title: Reshape AST to use Bindlib mbinders; rewrite walkers

files.description: Change EForall / EExists / EEach to carry (expr, guarded_body) Binder.Mbinder.t plus a param_meta list for pretty-printing. Introduce param_meta = { name : lower_ident; type_expr : type_expr }. Drop `[@@deriving show, eq]` at the expr level and hand-write equal_expr / pp_expr (alpha-aware via unbind).
Build quantifier / comprehension AST nodes via Binder.Mbinder.bind over the parsed params.
Unbind quantifiers to recover user-facing names + types for printing.
Rewrite substitute_vars, substitute_guards, prime_expr, prime_guards, unprime_expr as thin wrappers over Binder.Mbinder.subst / msubst. collect_body_guards uses unbind to descend.
Rewrite free_vars over Binder.Mbinder.free_vars. bind_head_params delegates scope tracking to the new free_vars.
Update any direct pattern matches on EForall/EExists/EEach to unbind the mbinder.
Update quantifier type-checking to unbind and add bindings to the environment.
Update declaration-gathering to handle the new AST shape.
Emit quantifiers by unbinding and writing user-facing names (byte-compatible with existing snapshots).
Same as json_output.ml.
Rewrite normal-form transformations on quantifiers via unbind/bind.
If any rule-guard storage references raw binder params, adapt it to the new param_meta type.

changes: Redefine ast.ml expr: `EForall of (expr, guarded_body) Binder.Mbinder.t * param_meta list`; similarly for EExists and EEach; add `and guarded_body = guard list * expr`.
Introduce param_meta = { name : lower_ident; type_expr : type_expr } (mirrors param, kept for pretty-printing / error messages).
Drop `[@@deriving show, eq]` from the `expr` (and `guard`, `guarded_body`) type declarations — Bindlib's mbinder is opaque to ppx_deriving.

Hand-write `equal_expr : expr -> expr -> bool`: for EForall/EExists/EEach, unbind both sides with `Binder.Mbinder.unbind2` (or unbind + fresh-name rebind) and recurse, so alpha-equivalent expressions compare equal. For non-binder variants, delegate to the standard structural comparison.
Hand-write `pp_expr : Format.formatter -> expr -> unit`: for EForall/EExists/EEach, unbind and use param_meta to recover the user-facing names + type annotations.
Parser: in each quantifier production, allocate Binder.Var.t for each param, call Binder.Mbinder.bind, and attach the param_meta list alongside.
Pretty printer: replace pattern matches on EForall (ps, gs, body) with `let vars, (gs, body) = Binder.Mbinder.unbind mb in ...`; use param_meta to recover the type annotations.

smt_expr.ml: reimplement substitute_vars as a fold that walks the expression and calls Binder.Mbinder.subst on the quantifier nodes. Drop substitute_guards' bound-name bookkeeping — guards still need explicit threading, but the binder-level bookkeeping is gone.
smt_expr.ml: reimplement prime_expr similarly.
smt_doc.ml: free_vars = Binder.Mbinder.free_vars applied to the quantifier nodes plus a standard walk over non-binding constructors.
smt_doc.ml: bind_head_params uses the rewritten free_vars (delete the in-file scope-tracking helpers scope_quantifier / scan_guards).

check.ml / collect.ml / normalize.ml / json_output.ml / markdown_output.ml: update every match arm that destructured a quantifier, using Binder.Mbinder.unbind and converting the fresh Var.t values back to Lower identifiers via Binder.Var.name.

Run `dune build` — no warnings. Run `dune test` — all existing tests pass. If any snapshot drift occurs (e.g., JSON field order for binder names), update the snapshot and include the diff in the patch for review.
patches.spec:
module BINDLIB_MIGRATION_PATCH_3.

> Patch 3 reshapes binder sites to use Bindlib's mbinder. After
> this patch, substitution respects alpha-equivalence and free
> variables are computed by the library. The latent capture bug
> is closed as a consequence (tests activated in Patch 4).

Expr.
Subst.
substitute s: Subst, e: Expr => Expr.
alpha-equivalent? e1: Expr, e2: Expr => Bool.
---
> Substitution respects alpha-equivalence.
all s: Subst, e1: Expr, e2: Expr, alpha-equivalent? e1 e2 | alpha-equivalent? (substitute s e1) (substitute s e2).

patches.title: Activate capture-avoidance tests

files.description: Remove pending markers; implement test bodies against the Bindlib-backed AST.

changes: Replace each `raise (Failure "pending")` with the real test body.
Test 1 body: construct e with free var set {a}, call substitute_vars [b -> ...] e, assert result = e.

Test 2 body: construct EForall [y:Int] (EVar x), call substitute_vars [x -> EVar y], assert the result is alpha-equivalent to EForall [z:Int] (EVar y) (via Binder.Mbinder.eq or a manual alpha-equivalence check).
Test 3 body: QCheck2 property — generate two alpha-equivalent expressions e1, e2 and a substitution s, assert substitute_vars s e1 is alpha-equivalent to substitute_vars s e2.

Test 4 body: for each fixture in test/regression/, parse it, collect the StringSet produced by the rewritten free_vars, and compare against a frozen baseline captured from the legacy implementation at Patch 2 stub time. (The baseline file ships with Patch 2.)
Run `dune test` — all four tests pass.
testStubsImplemented: Smt_substitution > substitute_vars is identity on non-free names
Smt_substitution > substitute_vars avoids capture in (all y:T | x) case
patches.spec:
module BINDLIB_MIGRATION_PATCH_4.

> Patch 4 activates the capture-avoidance tests. Formal
> postconditions: substitution is the identity when the variable
> is not free, and is capture-free for the (all y:T | x) case.

Expr.
Var.
free? x: Var, e: Expr => Bool.
bound-by e: Expr, x: Var => Expr.
substitute x: Var, rep: Expr, e: Expr => Expr.
alpha-equivalent? e1: Expr, e2: Expr => Bool.
---
> Substitution is the identity when the variable is not free.
all x: Var, rep: Expr, e: Expr, free? x e = false | substitute x rep e = e.

> Capture-avoiding substitution.
all x: Var, y: Var, rep: Expr, e: Expr, free? x e, free? y rep = false | alpha-equivalent? (substitute x rep (bound-by e y)) (bound-by (substitute x rep e) y).

patches.title: Retire #110's alpha-rename helpers and consolidate

files.description: Delete rename_var_refs (line 178) and rename_guards (line ~247). Tighten substitute_vars docstring to cite Binder.Mbinder.

Delete alpha_rename_binders (line 17) and its call site in the check pipeline (~line 743). The quantifier-binder-shadows-declared-rule case is handled structurally by Bindlib's fresh-variable allocation.
Delete any vestigial scope-tracking helpers that Patch 3 already stopped calling.

changes: Delete rename_var_refs and rename_guards from lib/smt_expr.ml.

Delete alpha_rename_binders and its invocation in lib/smt.ml (~line 743). Verify the three regression fixtures from PR #110 (bug_nested_binder_collision.pant, bug_rename_app_head.pant, bug_rule_param_collision.pant) still pass — they should, because Bindlib allocates fresh binder atoms that cannot collide with declared rule names.
Grep for any vestigial `bound : string list` threading in walkers that the Bindlib-backed API no longer needs; remove.
Update docstrings on substitute_vars and prime_expr to cite Binder.Mbinder as the source of capture-avoidance (remove the Barendregt-at-callsite caveat).
Confirm `dune build` and `dune test` remain green.
patches.spec:
module BINDLIB_MIGRATION_PATCH_5.

> Patch 5 removes helpers made redundant by the migration.
> No new invariants; the contribution is a smaller attack
> surface for binder-related bugs.

Expr.
Subst.
substitute s: Subst, e: Expr => Expr.
alpha-equivalent? e1: Expr, e2: Expr => Bool.
---
> Substitution still respects alpha-equivalence after cleanup.
all s: Subst, e1: Expr, e2: Expr, alpha-equivalent? e1 e2 | alpha-equivalent? (substitute s e1) (substitute s e2).

testMap.testName: Smt_substitution > substitute_vars is identity on non-free names
Smt_substitution > substitute_vars avoids capture in (all y:T | x) case

mergabilityInsight: 5 of 5 patches are INFRA. The migration preserves observable behavior on every existing fixture — the latent capture bug that motivates the refactor is not triggered by any current input. Patches 2 and 3 can execute in parallel after Patch 1 lands (test stubs reference only the adapter module, not the reshaped AST). Patch 4 activates the tests once Patch 3 has landed. Patch 5 is an optional consolidation that depends only on Patch 3.
finalStateSpec:
module BINDLIB_MIGRATION.

> ══════════════════════════════════════════
> THE GUARANTEE
> ══════════════════════════════════════════
> After this gameplan, Pantagruel's AST binder sites (EForall,
> EExists, EEach) are represented by Bindlib's mbinder. Capture
> avoidance is enforced by the library; the hand-rolled walker
> family in lib/smt_expr.ml collapses to thin wrappers. The
> latent capture bug in substitute_vars is closed.

Expr.
Var.
Subst.
free? x: Var, e: Expr => Bool.
bound-by e: Expr, x: Var => Expr.
substitute x: Var, rep: Expr, e: Expr => Expr.
substitute-many s: Subst, e: Expr => Expr.
alpha-equivalent? e1: Expr, e2: Expr => Bool.
---
> Substitution is the identity when the variable is not free.
all x: Var, rep: Expr, e: Expr, free? x e = false | substitute x rep e = e.

> Capture-avoiding substitution.
all x: Var, y: Var, rep: Expr, e: Expr, free? x e, free? y rep = false | alpha-equivalent? (substitute x rep (bound-by e y)) (bound-by (substitute x rep e) y).

> Substitution respects alpha-equivalence.
all s: Subst, e1: Expr, e2: Expr, alpha-equivalent? e1 e2 | alpha-equivalent? (substitute-many s e1) (substitute-many s e2).

where

> ══════════════════════════════════════════
> REDUCED SURFACE
> ══════════════════════════════════════════
> The walker family (substitute_vars, prime_expr, free_vars)
> is implemented via Bindlib primitives. Helpers added by PR
> #110 (rename_var_refs, alpha_rename_binders) are removed.

SmtExpr.
hand-rolled-walkers s: SmtExpr => Nat0.
library-backed? s: SmtExpr => Bool.
---
> No hand-rolled capture-avoidance walkers remain.
all s: SmtExpr | library-backed? s.
all s: SmtExpr | hand-rolled-walkers s = 0.
